concept: Catch / Receive the Moving Thing / Close the Hand on What Arrives
description: To catch: the thrown rope, the falling cup, the fish in the net ('mera'). Receiving what moves — the completing half of 'wapho' (throw). Between them, things can now cross gaps in both directions.
sound_symbolism: Three beats of the event: 'ka' the contact, 'we' the incoming thing, 'pa' the hand closing around it. Arrival, met.
grammatical_notes: Transitive: 'mia suro kawepa.' (I catch the rope.) The save: 'shia soeli kawepa.' (They catch the cup.) With the net: 'lo mia roe mera shalu kawepa.' (We catch fish with the net.)

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Catching is receiving made active: attention extended toward what another sends. The verb of good listening, enacted.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The catcher completes every throw — harvest tossed from ladder to basket, rope from boat to dock. Cooperation with a gap in the middle.

semantic_domains.community: The other end of every throw
